cards.front: What is a profile in UNIX?
cards.back: A file that stores the current user's login information and settings.

cards.front: What does the ls -l -p option in UNIX do?
cards.back: Show symbolic links and other special files

cards.front: What is the purpose of the ~/.profile file?
cards.back: To specify commands to be executed when the user logs in

cards.front: What is the purpose of the ~/.bash_profile file?
cards.back: To specify commands to be executed when the user logs in and the shell is not already executing a command

cards.front: What happens if the ~/.profile file does not exist?
cards.back: The system uses the /etc/profile file instead
